trajectory_summary: This is a short re-booking call on a warm, cooperative customer who had missed or rescheduled a prior appointment. Brandon gets the time on the calendar efficiently, which is the primary job here. However, the call does zero qualification work — no verify items captured, no bill anchor, no curiosity created — leaving the closer walking in blind on a lead whose prior appointment already fell through. The appointment is booked but structurally thin.
outcome_dispute: If the v1 tag is 'appointment_booked' (clean), that is conditionally accurate on the narrow question of whether a time was set — Wednesday at 6:00 PM was confirmed. However, the booking is fragile: no verify completion, no bill captured, no emotional anchor, and a prior appointment that was already missed or rescheduled. The closer has no context. A clean booking tag overstates the quality of what was built here.

primary_coaching_focus.quote: Okay. Good deal. Okay. Great. Alright. Thank you, Brian. We'll go over our solar programs and show you how much you could be saving with solar energy.
primary_coaching_focus.why: Brian missed or rescheduled a prior appointment — the transcript is ambiguous on cause, but either way this is a customer who already had one appointment fall through. The button-up protocol (one-hour block, phone/computer anchor, 'is that fair' handshake, text promise) is the primary tool for protecting show rate on exactly this type of fragile re-booking. Brandon hit zero of the five required elements. This is the highest-leverage coaching gap on this call because the appointment is already at elevated no-show risk and nothing was done to mitigate it.

key_moments.quote: Hi. If you record your name and reason for calling, I'll see if this person is available.
key_moments.stewart_read: Brian has a screening system on his line — he is not a passive pickup. The fact that Brandon cleared it and Brian stayed on the line, then answered with 'Hey. What's up, buddy?' (00:27), is a materially stronger positive signal than a cold pickup. This customer remembers the prior contact and chose to take the call. That warmth is an asset Brandon never explicitly acknowledged or leveraged.

key_moments.quote: Just reaching back out about that design for your home. We didn't get a chance to meet with you last week, so I was calling to see if you'd be available. I have a specialist who can meet with you and help you explore your options.
key_moments.stewart_read: Brandon skips the entire verify section and goes straight to scheduling. The schema's short-call compression provision (call_shape note) allows compression when 'existing records cover qualification' — but that provision requires the rep to have confirmed those records are current and complete. On a recycled lead where the prior appointment was missed or rescheduled, the qualification state is stale by definition. The reactive read holds: this is not strategic compression, it is a skip. The strategic hypothesis — that Brandon had confirmed qualification from prior records and was deliberately compressing — is unsupported because nothing in the call indicates he checked or referenced any prior data.

key_moments.quote: And, you said Thursday works best?
key_moments.stewart_read: Brandon misheard Brian's constraint. Brian had said 'anything except Thursday' (01:02 context) and Brandon reflected it back as 'Thursday works best' — the opposite of what was said. Brian had to correct him at 01:18 ('Anything except Thursday'). This is an active-listening failure on a simple scheduling exchange. On a warm, cooperative customer it didn't derail the call, but it introduced a small trust friction and required the customer to do corrective work.

key_moments.quote: Okay. Good deal. Okay. Great. Alright. Thank you, Brian. We'll go over our solar programs and show you how much you could be saving with solar energy.
key_moments.stewart_read: Brandon confirms the appointment and delivers a generic value statement, but the button-up protocol is almost entirely absent. Missing: one-hour block stated, phone appointment specified, no-driving/at-computer anchor, 'is that fair' verbal handshake, and text follow-up promise. On a re-booked appointment where the prior sit was already missed or rescheduled, the button-up protocol is the primary tool for protecting show rate. Skipping it here is the highest-risk gap in the call.
